seoTitle: Narada Products | Lead-Acid Batteries, Lithium-Ion Batteries, Energy Storage
seoDescription: Browse Narada's comprehensive battery portfolio including VRLA lead-acid batteries, LiFePO4 lithium-ion batteries, and energy storage systems. View specifications and request quotations.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by Narada?
faqs.answer: Narada offers four main product categories: 1) Lead-Acid Batteries - including VRLA batteries for telecom, UPS, and industrial applications; 2) Lithium-Ion Batteries - including LiFePO4 battery systems for energy storage and EV applications; 3) Energy Storage Systems - integrated BESS solutions for grid and commercial applications; 4) Telecom Power Solutions - specialized batteries for telecommunications infrastructure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below to find the right solution for your application.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Narada battery for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Narada battery involves several steps: 1) Define your application requirements including capacity, voltage, and discharge rate; 2) Determine operating environment including temperature range and installation location; 3) Calculate backup time or cycle requirements; 4) Consider physical constraints such as size and weight; 5) Evaluate total cost of ownership including maintenance and replacement cycles. Our FAE team can provide detailed guidance and sizing calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our product selection tables below or contact our FAE team with your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Narada's lead-acid and lithium-ion batteries?
faqs.answer: Narada lead-acid batteries offer proven reliability, lower initial cost, and established recycling infrastructure. They are ideal for standby applications like UPS and telecom backup. Narada lithium-ion batteries provide higher energy density, longer cycle life (3000-6000 cycles), faster charging, and lower total cost of ownership for cyclic applications. LiFePO4 chemistry offers excellent safety and thermal stability. Choose lead-acid for cost-sensitive standby applications and lithium-ion for high-cycle or space-constrained applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for a detailed comparison based on your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: What capacity ranges are available for Narada batteries?
faqs.answer: Narada offers batteries across a wide capacity range: Lead-acid batteries from 7Ah to 3000Ah in various form factors including 12V, 6V, and 2V configurations. Lithium-ion batteries from 20Ah to 1000Ah modules, scalable to MWh-level systems. Telecom batteries typically range from 50Ah to 200Ah for standard applications. UPS batteries range from 7Ah to 200Ah for various backup time requirements. Custom configurations are available for large-scale energy storage projects.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check the specifications table below or contact us for custom capacity requirements.

faqs.question: Does Narada provide battery management systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, Narada provides comprehensive battery management systems (BMS) for lithium-ion battery solutions. The BMS monitors cell voltage, temperature, and current; provides cell balancing for optimal performance; protects against over-charge, over-discharge, and over-current; and communicates system status via CAN bus or RS485. For lead-acid batteries, Narada offers monitoring systems that track voltage, temperature, and conductance for predictive maintenance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for BMS specifications and integration guidance for your battery system.

categories.name: Lead-Acid Batteries
categories.description: Narada's lead-acid battery portfolio includes high-performance VRLA batteries for telecommunications, UPS, and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: Narada's lead-acid battery portfolio includes high-performance VRLA (Valve Regulated Lead-Acid) batteries designed for reliable backup power in telecommunications, UPS, and industrial applications. These batteries feature advanced AGM technology for superior performance, long service life, and maintenance-free operation. Available in various capacities and configurations to meet diverse application requirements. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive selection guidance and technical support for Narada lead-acid battery products.

series.name: HRL Series
series.description: High-rate long-life VRLA batteries for demanding applications

series.name: GPL Series
series.description: General purpose long-life batteries for standby applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Narada Lead-Acid Batteries
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right lead-acid battery based on capacity, voltage, and application requirements

faqs.question: How do I calculate the required battery capacity for my application?
faqs.answer: Battery capacity calculation involves: 1) Determine load current in amperes; 2) Specify required backup time in hours; 3) Calculate ampere-hours: Capacity (Ah) = Load Current (A) x Backup Time (h); 4) Add safety margin of 20-30% for aging and temperature effects; 5) Consider discharge rate - higher rates reduce effective capacity. For example, a 10A load requiring 4 hours backup: 10A x 4h = 40Ah, with 25% margin = 50Ah minimum.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed capacity calculations including temperature derating.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 10-year and 15-year design life batteries?
faqs.answer: Narada offers batteries with different design life ratings: 10-year design life batteries use standard alloy and construction, suitable for general-purpose applications with typical operating conditions. 15-year design life batteries feature advanced alloy formulations, thicker plates, and enhanced construction for longer service life. The 15-year series is recommended for critical applications, remote sites with limited access, or installations where replacement is difficult or costly.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 15-year design life for critical applications or difficult replacement scenarios.

faqs.question: How does temperature affect lead-acid battery performance?
faqs.answer: Temperature significantly affects lead-acid battery performance: Higher temperatures (above 25C) increase capacity but accelerate aging - every 10C above 25C roughly doubles the aging rate. Lower temperatures reduce available capacity - at 0C, capacity may drop to 80% of rated. Optimal operating temperature is 20-25C. Narada batteries are rated at 25C, and derating should be applied for other temperatures. High-temperature versions are available for harsh environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Install batteries in temperature-controlled environments when possible. Contact us for high-temperature options.

faqs.question: What maintenance is required for Narada VRLA batteries?
faqs.answer: Narada VRLA batteries are designed to be maintenance-free under normal operating conditions. However, recommended practices include: 1) Regular visual inspection for physical damage or leakage; 2) Quarterly voltage checks to identify weak cells; 3) Annual impedance or conductance testing for capacity assessment; 4) Keep batteries clean and dry; 5) Ensure proper ventilation; 6) Maintain records of all measurements. No electrolyte addition or specific gravity checks are required for VRLA batteries.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement a regular monitoring program to detect issues before they become critical.

faqs.question: Can Narada lead-acid batteries be used in cyclic applications?
faqs.answer: While Narada lead-acid batteries are optimized for standby applications, certain series can handle moderate cycling. The HRL (High-Rate Long-life) series offers improved cycle life for applications with occasional discharge cycles. For frequent cycling applications (daily cycles), lithium-ion batteries are strongly recommended as they offer 10-20x the cycle life of lead-acid. Contact our FAE team to discuss your specific cycling requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For daily cycling, consider Narada lithium-ion batteries. For occasional cycling, HRL series lead-acid may be suitable.

products.name: 12V 100Ah VRLA Battery
products.shortDescription: High-capacity 12V VRLA battery with 100Ah capacity, designed for telecom and UPS applications with 10-year design life.
descriptionParagraphs: The Narada 12V100AH is a high-performance VRLA battery designed for reliable backup power in telecommunications and UPS applications.
With 100Ah capacity and advanced AGM technology, this battery delivers consistent performance and long service life.
The maintenance-free design and sealed construction make it ideal for installations where regular maintenance is not practical.
features: Advanced AGM technology for superior performance

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The 12V100AH is one of our most popular battery models for telecom and UPS applications. In my experience supporting numerous installations, this battery consistently delivers reliable performance with actual service life often exceeding the 10-year rating when properly maintained. The M8 terminal design provides secure connections and the compact form factor fits standard 19-inch racks. I particularly recommend this model for base station applications where space is at a premium but reliability cannot be compromised.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable workhorse battery for telecom and UPS applications

alternativeParts.reason: Longer design life version for critical applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring extended service life or difficult replacement

companionParts.description: 19-inch rack for battery installation
Remote monitoring system for battery status

faqs.question: What is the maximum discharge current for the 12V100AH?
faqs.answer: The 12V100AH battery can support maximum discharge currents up to 300A for short durations (less than 1 minute). For sustained discharge, the recommended maximum is 50A to prevent excessive heating and maintain battery life. The internal resistance is approximately 4-5 milliohms, allowing high-rate discharge for UPS applications where brief high-current discharge is required.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-rate discharge applications, ensure adequate ventilation and consider the HRL series.

faqs.question: What charging voltage should be used for the 12V100AH?
faqs.answer: Recommended charging parameters: Float charge voltage: 13.5-13.8V at 25C; Equalization charge voltage: 14.4-14.7V; Temperature compensation: -3mV/C per cell. For cyclic applications, use 14.4-14.7V absorption voltage with current limited to 0.3C (30A for 100Ah battery). Always use temperature-compensated charging when batteries operate outside 20-30C range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use temperature-compensated charging for optimal battery life. Contact us for charger recommendations.

faqs.question: How should the 12V100AH be stored if not in use?
faqs.answer: For storage: 1) Charge battery fully before storage; 2) Store in cool, dry location (ideally 10-20C); 3) Store in open circuit (disconnected from load); 4) Recharge every 6 months if stored at 20C, more frequently at higher temperatures; 5) Self-discharge is approximately 3% per month at 20C. After storage, recharge fully before returning to service.
faqs.decisionGuide: For long-term storage, maintain regular charging schedule. Store in temperature-controlled environment.

faqs.question: What is the warranty period for the 12V100AH?
faqs.answer: The standard warranty for Narada 12V100AH is 3 years from date of manufacture for standby applications. Extended warranties may be available for specific projects. Warranty covers defects in materials and workmanship under normal use and service. Proper installation, charging, and maintenance according to Narada specifications are required for warranty validity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure proper installation and maintenance to maintain warranty coverage.

faqs.question: Can multiple 12V100AH batteries be connected in parallel?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple 12V100AH batteries can be connected in parallel to increase system capacity. Best practices: 1) Use batteries of same model, age, and state of charge; 2) Connect with equal length cables to ensure balanced current sharing; 3) Limit parallel strings to 4 maximum for optimal performance; 4) Use proper fusing for each parallel string; 5) Monitor individual string voltages to detect imbalances. For large systems, consider using 2V cells in series instead.
faqs.decisionGuide: For systems requiring more than 400Ah, consider 2V cells or contact us for system design assistance.

products.name: 2V 500Ah VRLA Battery
products.shortDescription: High-capacity 2V VRLA cell with 500Ah capacity for large telecom and industrial battery systems with 15-year design l...
descriptionParagraphs: The Narada 2V500AH is a high-capacity 2V cell designed for large battery systems in telecommunications and industrial applications.
With 500Ah capacity and 15-year design life, this cell provides reliable long-term backup power for critical infrastructure.
The 2V design allows flexible system voltage configuration from 24V to 480V DC.
features: High capacity 2V design for large systems

faeReview.author: Michael Wang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The 2V500AH is our go-to solution for large battery systems requiring 48V or higher DC voltages. The 2V cell design offers several advantages over 12V monoblocs: better cell balancing, easier maintenance, and longer service life. I've specified this cell for numerous telecom central office installations and data center UPS systems. The 15-year design life reduces total cost of ownership significantly compared to standard 10-year batteries.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent choice for large-scale battery systems requiring long service life

alternativeParts.reason: Higher capacity option for space-constrained installations
alternativeParts.useCase: Use when higher capacity per cell is needed to reduce installation footprint

companionParts.description: Rack system for 2V cell installation
Copper connectors for 2V cell interconnection

faqs.question: How many 2V500AH cells are needed for a 48V system?
faqs.answer: For a 48V system, you need 24 cells of 2V500AH connected in series (24 x 2V = 48V). For a 500Ah 48V battery bank, this would be 24 cells total. For higher capacity, multiple strings of 24 cells can be connected in parallel. For example, 1000Ah at 48V would require 48 cells (2 strings of 24 cells each).
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate system voltage requirement and divide by 2V to determine number of cells needed.

faqs.question: What is the recommended spacing between 2V cells?
faqs.answer: Recommended spacing for 2V cells: Minimum 10mm between cells for air circulation; 20-30mm preferred for better cooling. Allow minimum 100mm clearance above terminals for maintenance access. For rack-mounted installations, follow the rack manufacturer's spacing recommendations. Proper spacing is essential for heat dissipation and to prevent thermal runaway in case of cell failure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow installation manual spacing requirements. Ensure adequate ventilation in battery room.

faqs.question: Can 2V cells be mixed with different capacities in the same system?
faqs.answer: No, all cells in a series string must have the same capacity and be from the same production batch when possible. Mixing different capacities will result in uneven charging and discharging, reducing system performance and life. For parallel strings, each string should have the same total capacity. When expanding existing systems, add complete new strings rather than mixing old and new cells in the same string.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use identical cells in a series string. Contact us for system expansion guidance.

faqs.question: What torque should be used for 2V cell terminals?
faqs.answer: Recommended terminal torque for M10 terminals on 2V500AH cells: 15-20 Nm (11-15 ft-lbs). Use calibrated torque wrench and tighten in stages. After initial installation, retorque after 24 hours and again after one week of operation. Proper torque ensures low-resistance connections without damaging the terminal. Apply thin coating of petroleum jelly to prevent corrosion.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use calibrated torque wrench and follow retorque schedule for reliable connections.

faqs.question: How often should 2V cells be equalized?
faqs.answer: Equalization charging for 2V cells: For standby applications, equalize every 3-6 months or when cell voltage variation exceeds 0.05V. For cyclic applications, equalize monthly. Equalization voltage is typically 2.40-2.45V per cell (57.6-58.8V for 48V system) for 8-12 hours. Monitor cell temperatures during equalization and reduce voltage if temperature exceeds 45C.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement regular equalization schedule to maintain cell balance and extend battery life.

categories.name: Lithium-Ion Batteries
categories.description: Narada's lithium-ion battery portfolio features advanced LiFePO4 technology for high-cycle applications requiring superior energy density and long service life.
categories.longDescription: Narada's lithium-ion battery portfolio features advanced LiFePO4 (Lithium Iron Phosphate) technology designed for applications requiring high energy density, long cycle life, and fast charging capabilities. These batteries offer 3000-6000 cycle life, significantly outperforming lead-acid alternatives for cyclic applications. The built-in battery management system (BMS) ensures safe operation with comprehensive protection against over-charge, over-discharge, and thermal runaway. Ideal for renewable energy storage, electric vehicles, and commercial/industrial energy storage systems where space and weight are critical considerations.

series.name: NLP Series
series.description: LiFePO4 batteries for telecom and UPS applications

series.name: NESS Series
series.description: High-capacity lithium modules for energy storage systems

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Narada Lithium-Ion Batteries
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting LiFePO4 batteries based on capacity, cycle life, and application requirements

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Narada LiFePO4 batteries over lead-acid?
faqs.answer: Narada LiFePO4 batteries offer several key advantages: 1) Cycle life: 3000-6000 cycles vs 300-500 for lead-acid; 2) Energy density: 3-4x higher, reducing space and weight requirements; 3) Charging efficiency: 95%+ vs 70-80% for lead-acid; 4) Depth of discharge: Can discharge to 80-90% vs 50% recommended for lead-acid; 5) Maintenance: No maintenance required vs regular maintenance for lead-acid; 6) Temperature tolerance: Better performance across temperature range. The higher initial cost is offset by longer life and lower total cost of ownership for high-cycle applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications with daily cycling or space constraints, LiFePO4 offers superior value. Contact us for TCO analysis.

faqs.question: How do I size a lithium-ion battery system for solar storage?
faqs.answer: Sizing a lithium-ion battery system for solar involves: 1) Calculate daily energy consumption in kWh; 2) Determine desired days of autonomy (typically 1-3 days); 3) Account for depth of discharge (use 80% for LiFePO4); 4) Add inverter efficiency factor (typically 90-95%); 5) Include temperature derating if applicable. Formula: Battery Capacity (kWh) = Daily Consumption x Autonomy Days / (DoD x Inverter Efficiency). For example: 10kWh daily, 2 days autonomy, 80% DoD, 93% inverter efficiency: 10 x 2 / (0.8 x 0.93) = 26.9 kWh battery system needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed solar storage system sizing including load analysis and peak demand calculations.

faqs.question: What safety features are included in Narada lithium batteries?
faqs.answer: Narada lithium batteries include comprehensive safety features: 1) Battery Management System (BMS) monitors cell voltage, temperature, and current; 2) Cell balancing ensures uniform charge across all cells; 3) Over-charge protection prevents voltage exceeding safe limits; 4) Over-discharge protection prevents damaging deep discharge; 5) Over-current protection limits charge/discharge current; 6) Short-circuit protection with fast-acting fuses; 7) Thermal management with temperature sensors and cooling control; 8) Communication interfaces for remote monitoring. LiFePO4 chemistry is inherently safer than other lithium-ion chemistries with excellent thermal stability.
faqs.decisionGuide: All Narada lithium batteries meet UN38.3, UL1973, and IEC62619 safety standards. Contact us for certification details.

faqs.question: Can Narada lithium batteries be connected in parallel?
faqs.answer: Yes, Narada lithium battery modules can be connected in parallel to increase system capacity. Key considerations: 1) Use batteries of same model, capacity, and firmware version; 2) Connect all batteries to common bus with equal cable lengths; 3) Ensure all batteries are at similar state of charge before connection; 4) Use proper fusing for each parallel string; 5) Configure BMS for parallel operation; 6) Limit to 4-8 parallel strings for optimal performance. The built-in BMS will automatically balance current sharing between parallel strings. For large systems, consider using higher capacity modules instead of many parallel strings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for parallel connection guidelines and system design assistance for your specific application.

faqs.question: What is the warranty period for Narada lithium-ion batteries?
faqs.answer: Narada lithium-ion batteries come with a standard 5-year warranty for stationary applications and 3-year warranty for mobile applications. Extended warranties up to 10 years are available for qualifying projects. Warranty covers defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. Conditions for warranty validity include: proper installation according to Narada specifications, operation within specified temperature and voltage limits, use of approved charging systems, and regular monitoring via BMS. The warranty does not cover damage from abuse, improper installation, or operation outside specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for warranty terms and conditions specific to your application and location.

products.name: 48V 100Ah LiFePO4 Battery Module
products.shortDescription: High-performance 48V 100Ah LiFePO4 battery module with integrated BMS, designed for telecom and energy storage applic...
descriptionParagraphs: The Narada NLP48-100 is a 48V 100Ah LiFePO4 battery module delivering 4.8kWh of energy storage capacity.
With advanced cell technology and intelligent BMS, this module provides 4000+ cycles at 80% depth of discharge.
The compact 4U rack-mount design enables easy integration into standard telecom cabinets and energy storage systems.
features: LiFePO4 chemistry for superior safety and cycle life
Parallel connection capability up to 16 units

faeReview.author: James Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Energy Storage Systems
faeReview.content: The NLP48-100 has become my go-to recommendation for telecom and commercial energy storage applications. In field deployments, I've consistently seen these modules deliver on their 4000+ cycle life promise when operated within specifications. The integrated BMS with CAN communication makes system integration straightforward, and the parallel connection capability provides excellent scalability. I particularly appreciate the 4U rack-mount form factor which fits standard telecom infrastructure. For customers transitioning from lead-acid to lithium, this module offers a compelling value proposition with its 10-year design life and minimal maintenance requirements.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent choice for telecom and commercial energy storage with proven field reliability

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for smaller installations or when distributed battery placement is preferred

alternativeParts.reason: Higher energy density and extended cycle life for premium applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use when maximum cycle life and energy density are critical

companionParts.description: 19-inch rack for battery installation
Communication cable for BMS integration
Remote monitoring system for battery status

faqs.question: What is the recommended charging profile for the NLP48-100?
faqs.answer: The recommended charging profile for NLP48-100: 1) Constant Current (CC) phase: Charge at 0.5C (50A) until voltage reaches 58.4V; 2) Constant Voltage (CV) phase: Hold at 58.4V until current drops to 0.05C (5A); 3) Float phase: Maintain at 54.0V for standby operation. Temperature compensation: -3mV/°C per cell. The integrated BMS automatically manages the charging process and will disconnect if parameters are exceeded. Use only chargers designed for LiFePO4 batteries with appropriate voltage limits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for charger recommendations and charging profile configuration assistance.

faqs.question: How many NLP48-100 modules can be connected in parallel?
faqs.answer: Up to 16 NLP48-100 modules can be connected in parallel for a maximum system capacity of 76.8kWh (16 x 4.8kWh). For parallel connection: 1) All modules must have same firmware version; 2) Use equal length cables (within 5% length tolerance); 3) Connect to common DC bus with appropriate fusing; 4) Enable parallel operation in BMS configuration; 5) Commission all modules at similar SOC. The BMS automatically balances current between parallel modules. For systems requiring more than 16 modules, consider using higher voltage configurations or contact us for custom system design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for parallel connection guidelines and system architecture recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range for the NLP48-100?
faqs.answer: The NLP48-100 operating temperature range: Charging: 0°C to +55°C (derating above 45°C); Discharging: -20°C to +60°C (derating above 55°C); Storage: -20°C to +45°C (recommended -10°C to +35°C for long-term). Performance at temperature extremes: At 0°C, available capacity is approximately 90%; at -10°C, approximately 80%; at +55°C, charging current should be limited to 0.3C. The BMS includes temperature monitoring and will disable charging below 0°C to prevent lithium plating. For cold climate installations, consider battery heating systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for temperature derating curves and cold climate installation recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I monitor the battery status remotely?
faqs.answer: The NLP48-100 supports remote monitoring via: 1) CAN bus interface (CAN 2.0B protocol) for integration with inverters and energy management systems; 2) RS485 interface (Modbus RTU protocol) for building management systems; 3) Optional SNMP/HTTP gateway for network-based monitoring. Monitored parameters include: state of charge (SOC), state of health (SOH), cell voltages, temperatures, current, and alarm status. The BMS provides standard communication protocols and can integrate with most major inverter brands including SMA, Sungrow, and Victron.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for communication protocol specifications and integration assistance with your specific system.

faqs.question: What maintenance is required for the NLP48-100?
faqs.answer: The NLP48-100 is designed to be maintenance-free under normal operating conditions. Recommended practices include: 1) Regular visual inspection for physical damage or warning indicators; 2) Monitor BMS status via communication interface monthly; 3) Check and record SOC/SOH trends quarterly; 4) Verify tightness of electrical connections annually; 5) Keep battery clean and free from dust; 6) Ensure adequate ventilation and cooling; 7) Update BMS firmware when updates are available. No electrolyte checks, watering, or equalization charging is required. The BMS automatically handles cell balancing during normal operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for maintenance schedules and remote monitoring solutions.

products.name: 51.2V 200Ah Energy Storage System
products.shortDescription: High-capacity 10.24kWh LiFePO4 energy storage module for residential and commercial applications with 6000+ cycle lif...
descriptionParagraphs: The Narada NESS-10K is a high-capacity 51.2V 200Ah energy storage module delivering 10.24kWh of usable energy.
Designed for residential and commercial energy storage applications with outdoor-rated IP65 enclosure.
With 6000+ cycle life and intelligent thermal management, this system provides reliable long-term energy storage.

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Renewable Energy
faeReview.content: The NESS-10K is an impressive energy storage solution that I've specified for numerous residential and commercial solar installations. The 6000+ cycle life rating is conservative based on my field observations - many installations are tracking toward 7000+ cycles. The IP65 enclosure is a significant advantage, allowing outdoor installation without additional shelters, which reduces installation costs. The active thermal management maintains optimal cell temperature even in challenging climates. I particularly recommend this module for residential solar-plus-storage systems where the 10kWh capacity matches typical daily household consumption patterns.
faeReview.highlight: Outstanding energy storage solution with proven field performance and outdoor-rated design

alternativeParts.reason: Lower capacity option for indoor installations or smaller systems
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for smaller residential systems or indoor telecom applications

alternativeParts.reason: Higher capacity option for larger residential or commercial systems
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for larger homes or commercial applications requiring more storage capacity

companionParts.description: Hybrid inverter for solar and battery integration
Smart EMS for optimized energy dispatch
Wall or ground mounting bracket for NESS modules

faqs.question: What inverter brands are compatible with the NESS-10K?
faqs.answer: The NESS-10K is compatible with most major hybrid inverter brands through standard CAN/RS485 communication protocols. Verified compatible brands include: SMA (Sunny Island, Sunny Boy Storage), Sungrow (SH series), Victron (MultiPlus, Quattro), GoodWe (ET series), Solis (RHI series), and Deye (SUN series). The battery supports standard protocols including CAN 2.0B and Modbus RTU. For other inverter brands, contact us for compatibility verification. The BMS provides real-time SOC, voltage, current, and temperature data to the inverter for optimal charging and discharging control.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for specific inverter compatibility and integration guidelines for your project.

faqs.question: Can the NESS-10K be installed outdoors?
faqs.answer: Yes, the NESS-10K features an IP65-rated enclosure specifically designed for outdoor installation. The IP65 rating provides complete dust protection and protection against water jets from any direction. Operating temperature range for the outdoor enclosure is -10°C to +50°C. The enclosure includes integrated thermal management with fans for cooling and heaters for cold weather operation. Installation requirements include: level surface capable of supporting 95kg, adequate clearance for ventilation (minimum 200mm on sides, 300mm above), and protection from direct sunlight in hot climates. No additional shelter is required in most climates.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for outdoor installation guidelines and climate-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: How does the thermal management system work?
faqs.answer: The NESS-10K includes active thermal management to maintain optimal cell temperature: 1) Temperature sensors monitor cell temperature at multiple points; 2) Cooling fans activate when internal temperature exceeds 35°C; 3) Heating elements activate when temperature drops below 5°C; 4) BMS reduces charge/discharge current at temperature extremes; 5) Thermal insulation maintains stable internal temperature. The system is designed to maintain cell temperature between 15°C and 35°C for optimal performance and longevity. Power consumption for thermal management is typically less than 50W during active heating or cooling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for thermal performance data and climate-specific installation recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the expected calendar life of the NESS-10K?
faqs.answer: The NESS-10K has a 10-year calendar life design with 6000 cycle life at 80% depth of discharge. Calendar life is affected by: 1) Operating temperature - cooler temperatures extend calendar life; 2) Average state of charge - storing at 50% SOC extends life vs 100% SOC; 3) Cycling frequency - moderate cycling is better than no cycling or very heavy cycling. Under typical residential solar conditions (1 cycle per day), the battery will reach cycle life limit before calendar life. At 25°C average temperature and typical cycling, expected service life is 10-15 years. The BMS tracks both cycle count and calendar aging to estimate remaining useful life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for life prediction modeling based on your specific operating conditions.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the return on investment for a NESS-10K system?
faqs.answer: ROI calculation for NESS-10K involves several factors: 1) Initial system cost including battery, inverter, and installation; 2) Energy savings from self-consumption of solar generation; 3) Peak shaving savings if on time-of-use rates; 4) Backup power value (insurance against outages); 5) Incentives and rebates available in your area; 6) Battery degradation over time (typically 80% capacity after 6000 cycles). Example ROI calculation: $8,000 system cost, $1,200/year energy savings, $500/year peak shaving = $1,700/year total savings = 4.7 year payback. With 10+ year life, this provides 5+ years of positive returns. Contact us for detailed financial modeling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed ROI analysis including your local electricity rates and solar production data.

categories.name: Energy Storage Systems
categories.description: Narada's integrated energy storage systems (ESS) provide turnkey solutions for grid-scale, commercial, and industrial energy storage applications.
categories.longDescription: Narada's integrated energy storage systems (ESS) provide turnkey solutions for grid-scale, commercial, and industrial energy storage applications. These pre-engineered systems include battery modules, power conversion systems (PCS), energy management systems (EMS), and thermal management in standardized containerized enclosures. Available in configurations from 100kWh to multi-MWh, these systems support applications including peak shaving, load shifting, renewable integration, frequency regulation, and backup power. All systems comply with relevant grid codes and safety standards including UL9540, IEC62933, and UN38.3.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Narada Energy Storage Systems
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting ESS based on capacity, power requirements, and grid connection specifications

faqs.question: What are the main applications for Narada energy storage systems?
faqs.answer: Narada energy storage systems support diverse applications: 1) Peak shaving - reducing demand charges by discharging during peak periods; 2) Load shifting - storing low-cost energy for use during high-cost periods; 3) Renewable integration - smoothing solar/wind output and enabling higher renewable penetration; 4) Frequency regulation - providing grid stabilization services; 5) Backup power - providing uninterruptible power for critical loads; 6) Microgrids - enabling off-grid or islanded operation; 7) Voltage support - maintaining power quality at distribution level. Each application has specific power and energy requirements that determine optimal system sizing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to identify the best applications for your specific site and economic drivers.

faqs.question: How do I size an energy storage system for my facility?
faqs.answer: Sizing an ESS involves analyzing: 1) Energy requirements (kWh) - determine daily energy throughput needed; 2) Power requirements (kW) - identify peak discharge/charge power needed; 3) Duration - calculate required discharge duration (hours = kWh/kW); 4) Duty cycle - understand daily cycling requirements; 5) Grid connection - available interconnection capacity; 6) Physical constraints - available space for installation. Typical commercial sizing: Peak shaving systems are often sized for 2-4 hour duration; Solar-plus-storage typically 1:1 to 2:1 solar-to-storage ratio; Backup systems sized for critical load kW and required backup duration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for detailed site analysis and ESS sizing optimization including financial modeling.

faqs.question: What grid codes and standards do Narada ESS comply with?
faqs.answer: Narada energy storage systems comply with major international standards: IEC 62933 (electrochemical energy storage systems), UL 9540 (energy storage systems), UL 1973 (batteries for stationary applications), IEC 62619 (safety requirements for lithium batteries), UN 38.3 (lithium battery transportation), IEEE 1547 (interconnection standards), and relevant grid codes including CAISO, ERCOT, and regional requirements. Compliance certifications vary by product and region. All systems include necessary protection relays, monitoring, and control systems for grid interconnection. Contact us for specific certification documentation for your project location.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for certification documentation and compliance verification for your specific grid connection requirements.

faqs.question: What is included in a Narada turnkey ESS?
faqs.answer: Narada turnkey ESS includes: 1) Battery energy storage - lithium-ion battery modules with integrated BMS; 2) Power conversion system (PCS) - bidirectional inverters for AC/DC conversion; 3) Energy management system (EMS) - control software for system optimization; 4) Thermal management - HVAC or thermal conditioning for batteries; 5) Fire suppression - detection and suppression systems per NFPA 855; 6) Container/enclosure - weatherproof housing with security features; 7) Switchgear and protection - breakers, fuses, and protection relays; 8) Monitoring and communication - remote monitoring platform and SCADA integration. Optional additions include transformers, MV switchgear, and solar integration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for detailed system specifications and customization options for your project requirements.

faqs.question: How long does ESS installation and commissioning take?
faqs.answer: ESS installation timeline varies by system size: Small commercial systems (100-500kWh): 2-4 weeks including civil work, electrical installation, and commissioning. Medium commercial systems (500kWh-2MWh): 4-8 weeks including site preparation, container placement, electrical connection, and commissioning. Large utility systems (2MWh+): 8-16 weeks including extensive civil work, medium voltage infrastructure, and comprehensive testing. Timeline factors: Site preparation requirements, electrical infrastructure upgrades needed, permitting and interconnection approval, utility interconnection process, and commissioning test requirements. Narada provides project management support throughout the installation process.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for detailed project timelines and installation planning for your specific project.

products.name: 500kWh Containerized Energy Storage System
products.shortDescription: Turnkey 500kWh containerized ESS with 250kW PCS, integrated EMS, and outdoor-rated enclosure for commercial and indus...
descriptionParagraphs: The Narada ESS-500K is a turnkey 500kWh containerized energy storage system designed for commercial and industrial applications.
The system includes integrated lithium-ion batteries, 250kW bidirectional PCS, energy management system, and thermal management in a 20-foot container.
Pre-engineered and factory-tested for rapid deployment and reliable operation in peak shaving and renewable integration applications.

faeReview.author: Steven Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Grid Storage Solutions
faeReview.content: The ESS-500K represents an excellent balance of capacity, power, and cost for commercial applications. I've worked on numerous installations where this system delivered significant demand charge savings for industrial customers. The factory integration and testing reduces field commissioning time significantly - most sites are operational within 2-3 weeks of delivery. The integrated EMS provides sophisticated control strategies including peak shaving, time-of-use optimization, and solar self-consumption. I particularly appreciate the comprehensive safety systems including fire suppression and gas detection. For customers new to energy storage, this turnkey approach eliminates many of the integration risks.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent turnkey solution for commercial energy storage with rapid deployment capability

Medium voltage switchgear for utility interconnection
SCADA system integration for utility control

faqs.question: What control strategies does the ESS-500K EMS support?
faqs.answer: The ESS-500K EMS supports multiple control strategies: 1) Peak shaving - automatically discharging to reduce demand charges during peak periods; 2) Time-of-use optimization - charging during low-cost periods and discharging during high-cost periods; 3) Solar self-consumption - maximizing use of on-site solar generation; 4) Demand response - responding to utility signals for load reduction; 5) Frequency regulation - providing grid stabilization services (where permitted); 6) Backup power - providing uninterruptible power for critical loads during outages. The EMS can combine multiple strategies with user-defined priorities and schedules. Control parameters are configurable via web interface or API integration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for EMS capabilities and control strategy configuration for your specific application.

faqs.question: How is the ESS-500K connected to the electrical system?
faqs.answer: The ESS-500K connects at 480V AC three-phase and requires: 1) Main breaker - appropriately sized disconnect; 2) Utility interconnection - compliance with local utility requirements; 3) Protection relays - overcurrent, ground fault, and arc flash protection; 4) Metering - revenue-grade metering for utility interconnection; 5) Communication - internet connection for remote monitoring. For medium voltage grid connection, a step-up transformer is required. The system includes all internal switchgear and protection. External electrical work typically includes conduit runs, main disconnect, and utility coordination. Narada provides electrical single-line drawings and coordination studies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for electrical connection requirements and integration engineering support.

faqs.question: What maintenance is required for the ESS-500K?
faqs.answer: The ESS-500K maintenance program includes: Monthly - remote monitoring review, alarm verification, performance data analysis; Quarterly - visual inspection, HVAC filter replacement, connection torque checks; Annually - comprehensive system testing, protection relay testing, fire suppression inspection, calibration verification; 5-year - major component inspection, battery capacity testing, PCS maintenance. The system includes predictive maintenance algorithms that identify potential issues before they cause failures. Remote monitoring allows most diagnostics to be performed without site visits. Typical annual maintenance cost is 1-2% of system cost. Narada offers maintenance contracts with guaranteed uptime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for maintenance programs and service contracts for your ESS installation.

faqs.question: What is the round-trip efficiency of the ESS-500K?
faqs.answer: The ESS-500K achieves ≥ 88% round-trip efficiency (AC to battery to AC) under standard test conditions. Efficiency breakdown: PCS efficiency ~96% (each direction), battery charge efficiency ~95%, battery discharge efficiency ~98%, auxiliary loads (thermal management, controls) ~2-3%. Efficiency varies with operating conditions: Higher at partial load (up to 90% at 50% power), slightly lower at full power, temperature dependent (optimal at 20-25°C). Annual energy throughput efficiency accounts for standby losses and typically averages 85-87%. The EMS optimizes efficiency by managing operating temperature and charge/discharge rates.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for efficiency curves and annual energy modeling for your specific operating profile.

faqs.question: How does the ESS-500K handle grid outages?
faqs.answer: The ESS-500K provides backup power capability with < 100ms transfer time. During grid outage: 1) System detects loss of grid and islanding condition; 2) PCS switches to off-grid mode; 3) Battery discharges to support critical loads; 4) System maintains voltage and frequency for connected loads. Backup duration depends on load power and battery SOC - at 250kW load with 100% SOC, provides approximately 2 hours backup. The EMS can prioritize loads and shed non-critical loads to extend backup duration. When grid returns, system automatically resynchronizes and reconnects. For extended backup requirements, the system can integrate with backup generators.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for backup power sizing and critical load analysis for your facility.

products.name: 2MWh Utility-Scale Energy Storage System
products.shortDescription: Large-scale 2MWh containerized ESS with 1MW PCS for utility grid services, renewable integration, and wholesale energ...
descriptionParagraphs: The Narada ESS-2M is a utility-scale 2MWh containerized energy storage system designed for grid services and renewable integration.
The system delivers 1MW continuous power with 2-hour duration, ideal for frequency regulation, capacity services, and solar/wind firming.
Compliant with major grid codes including CAISO, ERCOT, and PJM requirements for wholesale market participation.

faeReview.author: William Park
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Utility Solutions
faeReview.content: The ESS-2M is designed specifically for utility-scale applications and wholesale market participation. I've supported multiple deployments for frequency regulation and solar firming projects. The < 50ms response time meets the stringent requirements of fast frequency response markets. The multi-layer control architecture enables simultaneous participation in multiple revenue streams - capacity, energy, and ancillary services. The system has demonstrated exceptional availability (> 98%) in field operations. I particularly recommend this system for IPPs and utilities looking to participate in wholesale markets. The comprehensive grid code compliance reduces interconnection approval time significantly.
faeReview.highlight: Purpose-built for utility applications with comprehensive market participation capabilities

Medium voltage switchgear for utility interconnection

faqs.question: What wholesale markets can the ESS-2M participate in?
faqs.answer: The ESS-2M can participate in multiple wholesale markets: 1) Frequency regulation markets - fast response (reg-up/reg-down) in CAISO, ERCOT, PJM, NYISO, ISO-NE; 2) Capacity markets - resource adequacy in PJM, NYISO, ISO-NE; 3) Energy arbitrage - buying low, selling high in day-ahead and real-time markets; 4) Ancillary services - spinning reserves, non-spinning reserves; 5) Demand response - emergency load reduction programs. Market participation requires: appropriate interconnection agreements, market registration, telemetry and communication systems, and bidding/scheduling software. The EMS includes interfaces for major market platforms and can automate bidding strategies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for market participation analysis and revenue optimization for your specific location.

faqs.question: How does the ESS-2M support renewable integration?
faqs.answer: The ESS-2M supports renewable integration through multiple functions: 1) Output smoothing - reducing solar/wind output variability with < 1-minute response; 2) Ramp rate control - limiting power change rates to meet interconnection requirements; 3) Firming - providing guaranteed capacity during peak periods; 4) Curtailment reduction - storing excess generation that would otherwise be curtailed; 5) Forecast error correction - responding to generation forecast deviations; 6) Evening peak shaving - discharging to cover evening peak after solar production declines. The system can be AC-coupled (standalone) or DC-coupled (integrated with solar inverters). Typical solar-to-storage ratios range from 1:1 to 4:1 depending on application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for renewable integration design and solar-plus-storage optimization.

faqs.question: What grid support functions does the ESS-2M provide?
faqs.answer: The ESS-2M provides comprehensive grid support functions: 1) Frequency response - fast active power response to frequency deviations (droop control, synthetic inertia); 2) Voltage support - reactive power injection/absorption for voltage regulation; 3) Black start capability - system restoration support (optional); 4) Islanding support - intentional islanding for microgrid operation; 5) Harmonic filtering - active harmonic compensation; 6) Power quality - voltage sag/swell ride-through. Grid support functions comply with IEEE 1547-2018, UL 1741 SA, and regional interconnection requirements. Control parameters are configurable to meet specific utility requirements. The system has demonstrated successful operation in weak grid conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for grid support function specifications and interconnection requirements.

faqs.question: How is the ESS-2M monitored and controlled?
faqs.answer: The ESS-2M includes comprehensive monitoring and control: 1) Local HMI - touchscreen interface for local operation and diagnostics; 2) Remote monitoring - web-based dashboard accessible via secure internet connection; 3) SCADA integration - DNP3, Modbus TCP, and IEC 61850 protocols for utility control centers; 4) API interface - RESTful API for third-party integration; 5) Mobile app - iOS/Android app for status monitoring and alerts. Monitored parameters include: power, energy, SOC, voltage, current, temperature, alarms, and performance metrics. Control capabilities include: start/stop, power setpoints, mode selection, and schedule management. Data logging includes 1-second resolution for power and 1-minute for other parameters with 10+ year retention.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for monitoring and control specifications and integration requirements.

faqs.question: What is the warranty for the ESS-2M?
faqs.answer: The ESS-2M comes with a comprehensive warranty package: Battery warranty - 10 years or 6000 cycles at 80% capacity retention; PCS warranty - 10 years for power conversion system; System warranty - 5 years for balance of system components; Performance guarantee - guaranteed round-trip efficiency and availability. Warranty conditions include: operation within specified parameters, proper maintenance per Narada guidelines, use of approved service providers for repairs, and compliance with grid interconnection requirements. Extended warranties and performance guarantees are available. Insurance requirements typically include property and liability coverage. Narada provides warranty administration and claims processing support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for detailed warranty terms, conditions, and insurance requirements.

categories.name: Telecom Power Solutions
categories.description: Narada's telecom power solutions include specialized batteries, power systems, and integrated solutions designed specifically for telecommunications infrastructure.
categories.longDescription: Narada's telecom power solutions include specialized batteries, power systems, and integrated solutions designed specifically for telecommunications infrastructure. These products are engineered to meet the demanding requirements of telecom applications including long backup times, high reliability, wide temperature operation, and minimal maintenance. The portfolio includes front-terminal batteries for cabinet installations, high-temperature batteries for harsh environments, and integrated power systems with rectifiers, controllers, and distribution. All products comply with telecom industry standards and are deployed by major operators worldwide.

series.name: TEL Series
series.description: Front-terminal batteries designed for telecom cabinet installations

series.name: HTB Series
series.description: High-temperature batteries for outdoor and harsh environment applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Narada Telecom Power Solutions
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting telecom batteries based on backup time, temperature, and installation requirements

faqs.question: What battery types are best for telecom base stations?
faqs.answer: Telecom base station battery selection depends on site conditions: 1) Indoor climate-controlled sites - standard VRLA batteries offer lowest cost; 2) Outdoor cabinets - front-terminal VRLA batteries for easy maintenance access; 3) High-temperature sites (> 30°C average) - high-temperature VRLA or lithium-ion batteries; 4) Remote sites with limited access - long-life (15-year) batteries or lithium-ion; 5) Sites with frequent outages - lithium-ion for deep cycling capability. For 5G deployments with higher power requirements, lithium-ion is increasingly preferred due to higher energy density and longer life. Narada offers all these options with telecom-specific features.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for site assessment and battery selection based on your specific conditions.

faqs.question: How do I calculate battery capacity for telecom backup?
faqs.answer: Telecom battery sizing calculation: 1) Determine load current - sum of all equipment currents (typically 20-100A for macro sites); 2) Specify required backup time - typically 2-8 hours depending on grid reliability and generator availability; 3) Calculate ampere-hours: Ah = Load Current (A) x Backup Hours; 4) Add aging factor - 25% for VRLA to account for end-of-life capacity; 5) Add temperature derating - consult curves for high-temperature sites; 6) Select standard capacity from available sizes. Example: 50A load, 4 hours backup, 25% aging margin: 50 x 4 x 1.25 = 250Ah minimum. Consider future load growth (typically 20-30%).
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for detailed battery sizing calculations including load analysis and growth projections.

faqs.question: What is the difference between front-terminal and top-terminal batteries?
faqs.answer: Front-terminal vs top-terminal batteries for telecom: Front-terminal advantages: 1) Maintenance access from front without removing batteries from rack; 2) Ideal for cabinet installations with limited top clearance; 3) Standard 19-inch rack mounting; 4) Easier voltage monitoring and testing. Top-terminal advantages: 1) Lower cost for same capacity; 2) More compact footprint; 3) Shorter current path for high-rate discharge; 4) Traditional design with widespread availability. Front-terminal is preferred for telecom cabinets and outdoor enclosures where maintenance access is limited. Top-terminal is suitable for battery rooms with good access. Narada offers both types with telecom-optimized designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose front-terminal for cabinet installations, top-terminal for battery rooms. Contact us for installation guidance.

faqs.question: How do high-temperature batteries differ from standard batteries?
faqs.answer: High-temperature batteries for telecom applications feature: 1) Enhanced alloy formulations that resist corrosion at high temperatures; 2) Thicker plates for longer life under thermal stress; 3) Modified separators for improved electrolyte retention; 4) Higher electrolyte density for improved performance; 5) Special vent designs for water conservation. Performance at 35°C: Standard batteries lose ~50% life vs 25°C rating; High-temperature batteries lose only ~20% life. This makes them ideal for outdoor cabinets in hot climates where cooling is limited. The trade-off is slightly higher cost (10-20%) vs standard batteries, but significantly lower total cost of ownership in high-temperature applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use high-temperature batteries for sites with average temperature > 30°C. Contact us for temperature assessment.

faqs.question: What maintenance is required for telecom battery systems?
faqs.answer: Telecom battery maintenance best practices: Monthly - visual inspection via remote monitoring, alarm check; Quarterly - site visit for physical inspection, voltage check on sample cells, connection torque verification, temperature verification; Annually - comprehensive testing including impedance testing, capacity test on sample batteries, detailed connection inspection, cleaning. VRLA batteries are maintenance-free regarding electrolyte but require monitoring. Lithium-ion batteries require minimal maintenance with BMS handling cell balancing. Remote monitoring systems can reduce site visits by providing early warning of issues. Proper maintenance extends battery life and prevents unexpected failures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for maintenance programs and remote monitoring solutions for your telecom battery systems.

products.name: 12V 150Ah Front Terminal Telecom Battery
products.shortDescription: Front-terminal VRLA battery designed for 19-inch telecom cabinets with 12-year design life and narrow profile for spa...
descriptionParagraphs: The Narada TEL12-150FT is a front-terminal VRLA battery specifically designed for telecommunications cabinet installations.
The narrow 125mm width fits standard 19-inch racks while front terminals enable easy maintenance access without removing batteries.
With 150Ah capacity and 12-year design life, this battery provides reliable backup power for base stations and network equipment.
features: Front terminal design for easy maintenance

faeReview.author: Kevin Zhao
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Telecom Power
faeReview.content: The TEL12-150FT is specifically designed for telecom cabinet applications and addresses the key challenges of space constraints and maintenance access. The front-terminal design is a game-changer for cabinet installations - technicians can perform voltage checks and maintenance without removing batteries from the rack, saving significant time and reducing risk of connection damage. The narrow form factor allows efficient use of 19-inch rack space. I've specified these batteries for numerous 4G and 5G base station deployments. The 12-year design life aligns well with typical telecom equipment refresh cycles. For outdoor cabinet applications, I recommend pairing with the high-temperature version for extended life in challenging climates.
faeReview.highlight: Purpose-built for telecom cabinets with maintenance-friendly front-terminal design

alternativeParts.reason: Lower capacity option for smaller loads or shorter backup requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for small cell sites or locations with shorter backup time requirements

alternativeParts.reason: High-temperature version for outdoor cabinets in hot climates
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for outdoor sites with average temperature > 30°C

companionParts.description: Rack for cabinet installation of front-terminal batteries
Fused disconnect for battery protection
Remote temperature monitoring for battery management

faqs.question: How many TEL12-150FT batteries fit in a standard 19-inch rack?
faqs.answer: The TEL12-150FT fits standard 19-inch (482mm) racks with 125mm width per battery. In a typical 800mm deep rack: 6 batteries fit side-by-side per shelf (6 x 125mm = 750mm, leaving room for mounting). With 311mm depth, standard 600mm deep racks can accommodate the batteries with cable clearance. Typical configurations: Small sites - 1 shelf (6 batteries = 72V 150Ah); Medium sites - 2 shelves (12 batteries = 48V 300Ah or 24V 600Ah); Large sites - multiple racks. The 551mm height (including terminals) allows multiple shelves per rack. Always maintain minimum 10mm spacing between batteries for air circulation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for rack configuration recommendations and cabinet layout optimization.

faqs.question: What is the recommended charging voltage for the TEL12-150FT?
faqs.answer: Recommended charging parameters for TEL12-150FT: Float voltage: 13.5-13.8V per battery at 25°C; Equalization voltage: 14.4-14.7V per battery; Temperature compensation: -3mV/°C per cell (total -18mV/°C per 12V battery). For 48V systems (4 batteries in series): Float: 54.0-55.2V; Equalization: 57.6-58.8V. Charging current limit: 0.3C (45A maximum). The telecom rectifier should be programmed with these values. High-temperature sites (> 30°C) should use temperature-compensated charging to prevent overcharging. The battery is compatible with standard telecom rectifiers from major manufacturers including Eltek, Eaton, and Huawei.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for rectifier programming guidelines and charging profile recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I perform maintenance on front-terminal batteries?
faqs.answer: Front-terminal battery maintenance procedure: 1) Visual inspection - check for case damage, leakage, or swelling through cabinet window; 2) Voltage measurement - measure total string voltage and individual battery voltage using front terminals without removing batteries; 3) Connection check - verify torque on front terminals (M8: 15-20 Nm); 4) Temperature check - verify battery temperature is within range; 5) Impedance testing - use front terminals for conductance/impedance testing; 6) Cleaning - clean terminals and case with dry cloth if needed. The front-terminal design eliminates the need to remove batteries from rack for routine maintenance, significantly reducing maintenance time and risk of connection damage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for maintenance procedures and testing equipment recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the expected service life in telecom applications?
faqs.answer: Expected service life for TEL12-150FT in telecom: Ideal conditions (20-25°C, float charge, occasional discharge): 10-12 years; Moderate conditions (25-30°C, occasional cycling): 8-10 years; Challenging conditions (30-35°C, frequent cycling): 6-8 years. Factors affecting life: Temperature - every 10°C above 25°C approximately halves life; Cycling - frequent deep discharges reduce life; Charging - overcharging or undercharging accelerates aging; Maintenance - poor maintenance leads to premature failure. For sites with high temperatures, consider the HT (high-temperature) version. End of life is typically defined as 80% of rated capacity. Regular monitoring helps predict replacement needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for life prediction modeling based on your site conditions and operating profile.

faqs.question: Can the TEL12-150FT be used in outdoor cabinets?
faqs.answer: The TEL12-150FT can be used in outdoor cabinets with considerations: Temperature - standard version rated for -20°C to +50°C operation; for consistently high temperatures (> 30°C average), use HT version; Humidity - battery case is sealed and suitable for humid environments; Ventilation - cabinet must provide adequate airflow to prevent heat buildup; Insulation - cabinet should be insulated in extreme climates; Heating - battery heating may be needed in cold climates (< -10°C). For outdoor applications, the battery should be installed in a weatherproof cabinet with IP rating appropriate for the environment. The front-terminal design is particularly advantageous for outdoor cabinets where access may be limited.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for outdoor cabinet design recommendations and climate-specific solutions.

products.name: 12V 100Ah High Temperature Telecom Battery
products.shortDescription: High-temperature VRLA battery designed for outdoor telecom applications with enhanced performance at elevated tempera...
descriptionParagraphs: The Narada HTB12-100 is a high-temperature VRLA battery specifically engineered for outdoor telecom applications in hot climates.
Enhanced alloy formulations and specialized separators provide extended service life at temperatures up to 55°C.
With 100Ah capacity and optimized for high-temperature operation, this battery is ideal for outdoor cabinets and tower sites.

faeReview.author: Alex Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Harsh Environment Solutions
faeReview.content: The HTB12-100 is specifically designed for the challenging conditions of outdoor telecom sites, particularly in hot climates. I've seen standard batteries fail prematurely in outdoor cabinets in tropical and desert regions where internal temperatures regularly exceed 40°C. The HTB series addresses this with enhanced alloy formulations that resist corrosion at high temperatures. In field deployments, I've measured 30-40% longer service life compared to standard batteries in the same conditions. The trade-off is slightly higher initial cost, but the total cost of ownership is significantly better. I strongly recommend this battery for any outdoor installation where cooling is limited or unreliable. It's particularly popular in Southeast Asia, Middle East, and Africa deployments.
faeReview.highlight: Essential for hot climate outdoor installations with proven high-temperature performance

comparison.designLife: 10 years at 25°C vs 12 years at 35°C (HT better at high temp)

alternativeParts.reason: Standard version for climate-controlled indoor applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for indoor sites with air conditioning where temperature is controlled

alternativeParts.reason: Lithium-ion alternative for sites with frequent cycling or very long life requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for sites with unreliable grid requiring frequent deep cycling

companionParts.description: Forced ventilation for outdoor battery cabinets
Multi-point temperature monitoring for high-temperature sites
Reflective shield for cabinets in direct sunlight

faqs.question: How much longer does the HTB series last in high temperatures?
faqs.answer: HTB series life extension at high temperatures: At 35°C average temperature: Standard batteries lose ~50% of rated life; HTB batteries lose only ~20% of rated life. This translates to approximately 2-3 years additional service life in hot climates. At 40°C: Standard batteries may only achieve 3-4 years; HTB batteries achieve 6-8 years. The enhanced alloy formulation and specialized separators significantly reduce corrosion and water loss at elevated temperatures. For sites with average temperatures above 30°C, the HTB series typically provides lower total cost of ownership despite 15-20% higher initial cost. The payback period for the premium is typically 2-3 years.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for life-cycle cost analysis comparing standard and high-temperature batteries for your site conditions.

faqs.question: What temperature is considered high for telecom batteries?
faqs.answer: Temperature classifications for telecom batteries: Standard operation: 20-25°C (optimal), up to 30°C (acceptable); Elevated temperature: 30-35°C (HTB recommended); High temperature: 35-45°C (HTB strongly recommended); Extreme temperature: > 45°C (HTB essential, consider active cooling). Battery life impact: Every 10°C above 25°C approximately doubles the aging rate for standard batteries. HTB batteries are designed to maintain acceptable life at temperatures up to 45°C. For sites where cabinet temperature regularly exceeds 40°C, consider additional cooling measures or lithium-ion batteries which handle high temperatures better than VRLA.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for site temperature assessment and battery selection guidance.

faqs.question: How should HTB batteries be charged in high temperatures?
faqs.answer: High-temperature charging guidelines for HTB batteries: Temperature compensation is essential - reduce float voltage by 3mV/°C per cell for temperatures above 25°C. Example float voltages: At 25°C: 13.6V per battery; At 35°C: 13.4V per battery; At 45°C: 13.2V per battery. Use a rectifier with temperature compensation feature and place temperature sensor near batteries. Avoid charging above 55°C - disconnect batteries if temperature exceeds this limit. High-temperature charging without compensation leads to overcharging, accelerated water loss, and reduced life. The HTB design tolerates high temperatures better than standard batteries, but proper charging is still critical for maximizing life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for charging system configuration and temperature compensation setup.

faqs.question: Can HTB batteries be mixed with standard batteries?
faqs.answer: Mixing HTB and standard batteries in the same system is not recommended. Reasons: Different charging characteristics - HTB batteries may have slightly different voltage requirements; Different aging rates - batteries will reach end-of-life at different times; Impedance differences - can cause uneven current sharing; Warranty implications - mixing may void warranty. Best practice: Use all HTB or all standard batteries in a given system. If upgrading existing system, replace all batteries at once. For parallel strings, each string should use the same battery type. When replacing batteries in hot climate sites, consider upgrading the entire system to HTB for consistent performance and maximum life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for battery replacement strategies and system upgrade recommendations.

faqs.question: What other measures can extend battery life in hot climates?
faqs.answer: Additional measures for extending battery life in hot climates: 1) Cabinet design - use light-colored cabinets to reflect heat, ensure adequate insulation; 2) Ventilation - install forced ventilation or fans to exhaust hot air; 3) Shading - install sun shields or locate cabinets in shaded areas; 4) Thermal management - consider thermoelectric coolers for extreme climates; 5) Battery spacing - ensure adequate spacing between batteries for air circulation; 6) Monitoring - implement temperature monitoring with alarms; 7) Maintenance - more frequent inspections in hot climates; 8) Load management - reduce discharge rates during hot periods if possible. Combined with HTB batteries, these measures can achieve acceptable life even in challenging climates.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for comprehensive thermal management solutions for hot climate installations.
